parts.label: Display / Screen
parts.realLocation: Front, covering almost the entire face of the phone. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: It's the glass touchscreen you look at and tap every time you use the phone.

parts.label: Front (Selfie) Camera
parts.realLocation: Top edge of the front, usually a tiny hole or notch in the screen. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Points towards you for selfies and video calls.

parts.label: Earpiece / Speaker
parts.realLocation: Top edge, next to the front camera. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: The small speaker you hold to your ear during calls; bigger speakers also hide near the bottom edge.

parts.label: Sensors (Proximity/Light)
parts.realLocation: Hidden near the top edge, behind the screen glass. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Detects when the phone is near your ear (turns screen off) or how bright the room is.

parts.label: Motherboard
parts.realLocation: Right in the center, sandwiched between the screen and the back cover. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: The main circuit board that every other part plugs into — like the spine of the whole phone.

parts.label: CPU / Chipset
parts.realLocation: A small square chip soldered directly onto the motherboard. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: The 'brain' that does all the thinking — running apps, games, and calculations.

parts.label: RAM Chip
parts.realLocation: Stacked right next to or on top of the CPU chip. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Temporary fast memory the CPU uses while apps are open.

parts.label: Storage Chip
parts.realLocation: Another small chip next to the CPU on the motherboard. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Keeps your photos, apps and files saved even when the phone is off.

parts.label: Battery
parts.realLocation: A large flat pack taking up much of the lower-back half of the phone. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Stores the electrical energy that powers everything else.

parts.label: Main (Rear) Camera
parts.realLocation: Top-left corner of the back panel, often in a raised camera bump. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Takes the photos and videos you see on the back of the phone.

parts.label: Charging Port & IC
parts.realLocation: Bottom edge of the phone, where you plug in the charging cable. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Controls how electricity flows from the cable into the battery safely.

parts.label: Loudspeaker
parts.realLocation: Bottom edge, next to the charging port — tiny grille holes. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: The main speaker for music, videos, and ringtones.

parts.label: SIM Card Tray
parts.realLocation: On the side edge of the phone — a thin tray you eject with a pin. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Holds the tiny chip that connects you to your mobile network.

parts.label: Volume & Power Buttons
parts.realLocation: Right side edge of the phone. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Physical buttons to turn the screen on/off and adjust volume.

parts.label: Vibration Motor
parts.realLocation: A tiny cylinder tucked beside the battery. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Buzzes the phone for silent notifications and haptic feedback.

parts.label: WiFi / Antenna
parts.realLocation: Thin metal strips visible on the frame edges, often top and bottom. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Sends and receives WiFi and mobile signals through the metal frame.

parts.label: GPS Module
parts.realLocation: Small chip on the motherboard, wired to the antenna lines. (Typical modern smartphone layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Talks to satellites to figure out your exact location for maps.

parts.label: Display / Screen
parts.realLocation: The entire lid — the part that flips open. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Shows everything you're working on or watching.

parts.label: Webcam
parts.realLocation: Top edge of the screen bezel, centered. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Used for video calls and photos.

parts.label: Motherboard
parts.realLocation: Fills most of the base, underneath the keyboard. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: The main board connecting every part of the laptop together.

parts.label: CPU
parts.realLocation: Center of the motherboard, usually under a cooling fan/heatsink. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Processes everything you do on the laptop.

parts.label: Cooling Fan & Heatsink
parts.realLocation: Directly on top of the CPU, with vents on the side of the laptop. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Pulls heat away from the CPU so it doesn't overheat.

parts.label: RAM Sticks
parts.realLocation: Thin slots next to the CPU, sometimes accessible from the bottom panel. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Fast temporary memory for running programs.

parts.label: SSD / Storage
parts.realLocation: A small flat stick plugged into the motherboard, near the battery. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Stores your files, apps and operating system permanently.

parts.label: Battery Pack
parts.realLocation: A wide flat pack near the front edge, under the palm rest. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Powers the laptop when it isn't plugged in.

parts.label: Keyboard & Trackpad
parts.realLocation: The entire base you type and click on. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Your main way of typing and controlling the laptop.

parts.label: Speakers
parts.realLocation: Grille slots on either side of the keyboard, or underneath. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Produces the sound you hear.

parts.label: Charging Port
parts.realLocation: Side edge of the laptop base. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Where you plug in the power adapter to charge the battery.

parts.label: WiFi Antenna
parts.realLocation: Thin wires running along the top edge of the screen lid. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: The screen hinge area is often used for WiFi antennas since it's away from metal interference.

parts.label: Graphics Processor (GPU)
parts.realLocation: On the motherboard beside the CPU, normally under the same heat-pipe assembly. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Creates images, video, and 3D graphics. Some laptops use integrated graphics instead.

parts.label: Keyboard & Trackpad Ribbon
parts.realLocation: Flat ribbon cables run from the keyboard and trackpad through the upper base to motherboard connectors. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Carries your typing and trackpad signals to the motherboard.

parts.label: USB / HDMI Port Board
parts.realLocation: Along the left and right edges of the laptop base. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Connects chargers, USB devices, headphones, external screens, and other accessories.

parts.label: Display Hinge Cable
parts.realLocation: Runs through a hinge from the base to the screen lid. (Typical modern laptop layout; exact positions vary by model.)
parts.why: Sends video, webcam, and Wi-Fi antenna signals between the base and lid.
